kernel_read.positive: Group-owned accumulation has enough K16 fill at realistic group panels and can model 23.81x to 47.25x write reduction for token_block 32 or 64.
kernel_read.negative: A naive panel-owned kernel would rescan top-k once per group panel; group_panel 128/256/512 imply 8x/4x/2x rescan unless routes are prepacked or filtered inside the producer.
kernel_read.decision: Do not build a naive panel-rescan kernel. The next runtime kernel should use prepacked route ownership plus a group-owned K16 accumulator, or an equivalent in-producer segmented path.

recommended_first_runtime_rungs.reason: Lower-risk first S128/S512 bring-up; keeps token window aligned with the existing production target and avoids the wide-tile serialization seen in earlier reducers.
Higher write-reduction follow-up once the prepacked route-list and group-owned accumulator body work at smaller shapes.
Narrower group panel if accumulator pressure needs to drop, but only after route prepacking prevents 8x top-k rescans.

rejected_shortcuts: Do not interpret token_block=128/group_panel=1024 as a production target; it is an upper-envelope metadata point and risks repeating the wide-tile serialization failure.
Do not detach raw ScatterAdd again; it pays a full HBM partial round trip and already lost to the sparse-atomic gate.
Do not retry dense route materialization or group-major row-wise recompute; those branches were already slower by one to three orders of magnitude.
validation_gate: S128 parity against the current shared-KV dKV reference.
S512 timing against the active-list sparse-atomic gate before S4096.
S4096 timing must beat the 8.0-8.8 ms sparse-atomic dKV slice without materializing selected K/V, selected probabilities, dense route matrices, or multi-GiB partial slabs.
